elements.sent-2411-005: In 1968, Knuth \cite{knuth first introduced the stack sorting machine that at each step, either pushes the leftmost element of the input permutation to the stack or pops the top element of the stack onto the output.}
elements.sent-2411-006: In 1990, West \cite{west introduced a deterministic stack sorting map $s$ that drew inspiration from Knuth's non-deterministic stack sorting machine.}
elements.sent-2411-007: West's stack sorting map $s$ processes the input permutation through a stack in a right greedy manner such that elements of the stack increase from top to bottom (see for example, \Cref{fig1 ).}
elements.sent-2411-008: As hinted by the name, West's stack-sorting map eventually sorts all permutations after repeated iterations.
elements.sent-2411-009: A natural question then is how many permutations of length $n$ get sorted after $t$ iterations through West's stack-sorting map.
elements.sent-2411-010: As a first step to answering the question, West defined a permutation $\pi$ as $t-$stack sortable if $s^{t (\pi)$ is the identity permutation.}
elements.sent-2411-011: Subsequently, Knuth proved that a permutation is 1-stack-sortable if and only if it does not contain a subsequence that is order-isomorphic to 231.
elements.sent-2411-012: In addition, Knuth proved that the number of 1-stack-sortable permutations in $S_n$ is the $n^{\text{th }$ Catalan number $C_n = \tfrac{1 {n + 1}\tbinom{2n {n}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: In 1990, West \cite{west conjectured that the number of 2-stack-sortable permutations in $S_n$ is $\frac{2 {(n+1)(2n+1)}\binom{3n {n}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-014: West's conjecture was subsequently proved by Zeilberger \cite{zeil .}
elements.sent-2411-015: In 2021, Defant \cite{defant defined the dual of a $t-$stack sortable permutation as a $t$-sorted permutation for $t$ that is close to $n$.}
elements.sent-2411-016: A permutation is $t-$sorted if it is in the image of $s^t(S_n)$.
elements.sent-2411-017: In a related direction, Zhang \cite{zhang defined a minimally sorted permutation of $S_n$ to be a permutation that requires the maximum number of sorts through the stack-sorting map to be sorted.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Another chain of research extended West's stack-sorting map.
elements.sent-2411-019: In 2020, Cerbai, Claesson, and Ferrari \cite{stacksort generalized $s$ to $s_{\sigma}$, in which the stack must avoid the pattern $\sigma$ rather than descents.}
elements.sent-2411-020: In addition, Cerbai, Claesson, and Ferrari further extended pattern avoiding maps to $\sigma$ machines, which are composed of $s_{\sigma}$ followed by $s$.
elements.sent-2411-021: Berlow \cite{berlow extended $\sigma-$machines to maps avoiding two patterns and defined the $(\sigma,\tau)$-machine as the composition of $s_{\{\sigma,\tau\}}$ followed by $s$.}
